---
title: "Research"
title-block: false
---

# Research Agenda {#research-agenda}

I study how policies and institutions shape worker welfare, firm behavior, and the distribution of economic opportunity. Across projects, I combine applied microeconomic questions with quantitative methods that strengthen causal inference and improve evidence aggregation.

## Working Papers {#working-papers}

::: {.paper-stack}
::: {.paper-entry}
### Union Delegates Incentives and Firm-level Outcomes
<span class="paper-meta">with Francesco Armillei | Working paper</span>

We study how electoral accountability for workplace union representatives affects firm-level employment outcomes after a 2008 reform in France tied union legitimacy to vote shares and approval thresholds. The project examines labor earnings, hours worked, bargaining content, and within-firm inequality.
:::

::: {.paper-entry}
### Unconditional Cash Transfers: A Bayesian Meta-Analysis of 50 Randomized Evaluations in 26 Low- and Middle-Income Countries
<span class="paper-meta">with Dean Karlan, Finley Ong, Julius Ruschenpohler, and Christopher Udry | Working paper</span>

Using Bayesian meta-analysis, this project aggregates evidence from 115 studies of 72 unconditional cash transfer programs and evaluates how impacts vary across policy-relevant mechanisms and contexts.

::: {.link-row}
[Paper](https://www.nber.org/papers/w32779){.paper-link}
[Ideas for India](https://ideasforindia.in/topics/poverty-inequality/what-broad-lessons-have-we-learned-from-115-studies-on-unconditional-cash-transfers.html){.paper-link}
[VoxDev](https://voxdev.org/topic/social-protection/what-broad-lessons-have-we-learned-115-studies-unconditional-cash-transfers){.paper-link}
:::
:::
:::

## Talks / Press {#talks-press}

::: {.note-panel}
Selected external coverage of the cash transfer meta-analysis includes [Ideas for India](https://ideasforindia.in/topics/poverty-inequality/what-broad-lessons-have-we-learned-from-115-studies-on-unconditional-cash-transfers.html) and [VoxDev](https://voxdev.org/topic/social-protection/what-broad-lessons-have-we-learned-115-studies-unconditional-cash-transfers).
:::
